result.markdown:
--- title: "Initial QC & Data Cleaning — Proteome" --- ## Libraries ## Set directories ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} base_dir <- "/nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics" run_num <- "run1" results_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "results", run_num) objects_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "data", "processed", run_num) input_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "input") dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(objects_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) ``` ::: ## Define study design GA_1–4: hApp | GA_5–8: hAppNLGF | GA_9–12: hAppNLG_FIRE (no microglia) | GA_13–16: hApp_FIRE (no microglia) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} meta <- data.table( FileName = paste0("GA_", 1:16), Condition = rep(c("hApp", "hAppNLGF", "hAppNLG_FIRE", "hApp_FIRE"), each = 4), BioReplicate = rep(1:4, times = 4), Has_Microglia = rep(c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE), each = 4) ) meta ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Read Spectronaut MSStats proteome report Only the protein-level columns are selected to keep memory manageable on this 8.4 GB file. After deduplication the fragment-level rows collapse to one row per protein–sample pair. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Reading Spectronaut report (selecting protein-level columns only)...\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Reading Spectronaut report (selecting protein-level columns only)... ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} #no PG.Genes spec_raw <- fread( file.path(input_dir, "20260423_154110_PCF000612_GA_MSStats_Report-Proteome.csv")#, #select = c("R.FileName", "PG.ProteinGroups", "PG.ProteinAccessions", #"PG.Qvalue", "PG.Quantity") ) cat("Fragment rows loaded:", nrow(spec_raw), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Fragment rows loaded: 24057952 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Unique samples found:", uniqueN(spec_raw$R.FileName), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Unique samples found: 16 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Samples present:", sort(unique(spec_raw$R.FileName)), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Samples present: GA_1 GA_10 GA_11 GA_12 GA_13 GA_14 GA_15 GA_16 GA_2 GA_3 GA_4 GA_5 GA_6 GA_7 GA_8 GA_9 ``` ::: ::: ## Extract unique protein-level quantities per sample PG.Quantity repeats for every fragment of a protein within a sample — deduplicate to one row per protein–sample pair. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pg_long <- unique(spec_raw[, .(R.FileName, PG.ProteinGroups, PG.ProteinAccessions, PG.Qvalue, PG.Quantity)]) cat("Unique protein-sample rows:", nrow(pg_long), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Unique protein-sample rows: 142768 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Unique proteins:", uniqueN(pg_long$PG.ProteinGroups), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Unique proteins: 8923 ``` ::: ::: ## Pivot to wide format (proteins × samples) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} quant_wide <- dcast( pg_long[, .(R.FileName, PG.ProteinGroups, PG.Quantity)], PG.ProteinGroups ~ R.FileName, value.var = "PG.Quantity" ) sample_cols <- paste0("GA_", 1:16) # Sanity-check that all expected samples are present missing_samples <- setdiff(sample_cols, colnames(quant_wide)) if (length(missing_samples) > 0) { warning("These samples are missing from the data: ", paste(missing_samples, collapse = ", ")) } else { cat("All 16 samples found in data.\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` All 16 samples found in data. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # Extract quantitative matrix in numeric order (GA_1 … GA_16) quant_data <- quant_wide[, ..sample_cols] # Convert Spectronaut NaN to R NA throughout quant_matrix <- as.matrix(quant_data) quant_matrix[is.nan(quant_matrix)] <- NA cat("Proteins in matrix:", nrow(quant_matrix), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Proteins in matrix: 8923 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Samples in matrix: ", ncol(quant_matrix), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Samples in matrix: 16 ``` ::: ::: ## Calculate protein ID counts per sample A protein is "identified" if it has a finite, positive PG.Quantity. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} protein_counts <- colSums(!is.na(quant_matrix) & quant_matrix > 0) qc_counts <- data.table(FileName = names(protein_counts), Protein_Count = protein_counts) qc_data <- merge(qc_counts, meta, by = "FileName", all.x = TRUE) summary(qc_data$Protein_Count) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Min. 1st Qu. Median Mean 3rd Qu. Max. 8654 8697 8707 8711 8726 8765 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} qc_data ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## MAD outlier detection Flags samples with protein counts more than 3 MADs below the median. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} med_count <- median(qc_data$Protein_Count, na.rm = TRUE) mad_count <- mad(qc_data$Protein_Count, na.rm = TRUE) mad_cutoff <- med_count - (3 * mad_count) cat("Median Protein Count:", round(med_count), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Median Protein Count: 8707 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("MAD: ", round(mad_count), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MAD: 23 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("3-MAD cutoff: ", round(mad_cutoff), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 3-MAD cutoff: 8638 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} failed_runs <- qc_data[Protein_Count < mad_cutoff, FileName] if (length(failed_runs) > 0) { cat("\nWARNING:", length(failed_runs), "failed run(s) to drop:\n") print(failed_runs) } else { cat("\nNo runs fell below the 3-MAD threshold.\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` No runs fell below the 3-MAD threshold. ``` ::: ::: ## Generate PDF QC report ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} condition_colours <- c( "hApp" = "#1b9e77", "hAppNLGF" = "#d95f02", "hAppNLG_FIRE" = "#7570b3", "hApp_FIRE" = "#e7298a" ) # ── Plot 1: Protein Count per Sample with MAD cutoff ─────────────────────── p1 <- ggplot(qc_data, aes(x = reorder(FileName, Protein_Count), y = Protein_Count, fill = Condition)) + geom_bar(stat = "identity") + geom_hline(yintercept = mad_cutoff, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) + annotate("text", x = 2, y = mad_cutoff + 100, label = "3 MAD Cutoff", color = "red", hjust = 0) + scale_fill_manual(values = condition_colours) + coord_flip() + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Total Proteins Identified per Sample", subtitle = "Red dashed line = 3-MAD failure threshold", x = "Sample", y = "Protein Count") + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)) # ── Plot 2: Protein Yield by Condition ───────────────────────────────────── p2 <- ggplot(qc_data, aes(x = Condition, y = Protein_Count, fill = Condition)) + geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) + geom_point(aes(shape = Has_Microglia), position = position_jitter(width = 0.2), alpha = 0.8, size = 3) + scale_fill_manual(values = condition_colours) + scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 16, "FALSE" = 1), labels = c("TRUE" = "Microglia", "FALSE" = "No microglia")) + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Protein Yield by Condition", subtitle = "Filled circles = microglia; open circles = no microglia", x = "Condition", y = "Protein Count", shape = "Microglia present") # ── PCA ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── cat("Preparing PCA...\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Preparing PCA... ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} pca_mat_log <- log2(quant_matrix + 1) pca_mat_log_t <- t(pca_mat_log) noise_floor <- min(pca_mat_log_t[pca_mat_log_t > 0], na.rm = TRUE) / 2 pca_mat_log_t[is.na(pca_mat_log_t) | pca_mat_log_t == 0] <- noise_floor valid_cols <- apply(pca_mat_log_t, 2, var) > 0 pca_mat_log_t <- pca_mat_log_t[, valid_cols] cat("Proteins retained for PCA (non-zero variance):", sum(valid_cols), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Proteins retained for PCA (non-zero variance): 8792 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} pca_res <- prcomp(pca_mat_log_t, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE) var_expl <- round(100 * pca_res$sdev^2 / sum(pca_res$sdev^2), 1) pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x) pca_df$FileName <- rownames(pca_df) pca_df <- merge(pca_df, meta, by = "FileName", all.x = TRUE) # ── Plot 3: PCA by Condition ──────────────────────────────────────────────── p3 <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Condition, shape = Has_Microglia)) + geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85) + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = FileName), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) + scale_color_manual(values = condition_colours) + scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 16, "FALSE" = 1), labels = c("TRUE" = "Microglia", "FALSE" = "No microglia")) + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "PCA of Log2 Protein Intensities", subtitle = "Filled = microglia present; open = no microglia", x = paste0("PC1 (", var_expl[1], "%)"), y = paste0("PC2 (", var_expl[2], "%)"), shape = "Microglia present") # ── Save PDF ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── pdf_path <- file.path(results_dir, "Initial_QC_Report.pdf") cat("Saving PDF:", pdf_path, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Saving PDF: /nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics/results/run1/Initial_QC_Report.pdf ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} pdf(pdf_path, width = 10, height = 7) print(p1); print(p2); print(p3) dev.off() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` png 2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} print(p1); print(p2); print(p3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-2.png){width=960} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-3.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Abundance distributions Log2 intensity boxplots per sample, ordered numerically. Red outlines flag MAD failures. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} quant_log_plot <- log2(quant_matrix + 1) quant_log_plot[quant_log_plot == 0] <- NA abund_long <- as.data.frame(quant_log_plot) %>% mutate(Protein = row_number()) %>% pivot_longer(-Protein, names_to = "FileName", values_to = "Log2_Intensity") %>% drop_na() %>% left_join(as.data.frame(qc_data), by = "FileName") %>% mutate( Status = ifelse(FileName %in% failed_runs, "Failed (Outlier)", "Pass"), Sample_Num = as.integer(str_extract(FileName, "[0-9]+")) ) p_abund <- ggplot(abund_long, aes(x = reorder(FileName, Sample_Num), y = Log2_Intensity, fill = Condition)) + geom_boxplot(aes(color = Status), outlier.shape = NA, lwd = 0.4) + scale_fill_manual(values = condition_colours) + scale_color_manual(values = c("Pass" = "black", "Failed (Outlier)" = "red")) + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Log2 Abundance per Sample (All Proteins)", subtitle = "Red outlines = samples flagged by 3-MAD filter", x = "Sample (GA_1 → GA_16)", y = "Log2 Intensity") + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) print(p_abund) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=1152} ::: ::: ## Correlation heatmap Samples grouped by microglia status then condition to make the biological structure visible. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} quant_mat_cor <- log2(quant_matrix + 1) quant_mat_cor[quant_mat_cor == 0] <- NA cor_mat <- cor(quant_mat_cor, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") meta_sorted <- meta %>% arrange(Has_Microglia, Condition, BioReplicate) %>% as.data.frame() sorted_runs <- meta_sorted$FileName cor_mat_sorted <- cor_mat[sorted_runs, sorted_runs] anno_df <- meta_sorted %>% select(FileName, Condition, Has_Microglia, BioReplicate) %>% mutate(Has_Microglia = as.factor(Has_Microglia), BioReplicate = as.factor(BioReplicate)) %>% as.data.frame() rownames(anno_df) <- anno_df$FileName anno_df$FileName <- NULL pheatmap(cor_mat_sorted, main = "Pearson Correlation: Grouped by Microglia Status & Condition", annotation_col = anno_df, cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_cols = FALSE, show_rownames = TRUE, show_colnames = TRUE, color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(100), gaps_col = which(diff(as.numeric(as.factor(meta_sorted$Condition))) != 0), gaps_row = which(diff(as.numeric(as.factor(meta_sorted$Condition))) != 0)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=960} ::: ::: ## Average-correlation diagnostic Identifies the sample with the lowest mean correlation to the rest of the cohort. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} qc_diagnostics <- data.table( FileName = colnames(quant_mat_cor), Protein_Count = protein_counts[colnames(quant_mat_cor)], NA_Percent = colMeans(is.na(quant_mat_cor)) * 100, Avg_Correlation = rowMeans(cor_mat, na.rm = TRUE) ) outlier_id <- qc_diagnostics[which.min(Avg_Correlation), FileName] cat("--- Diagnostic for lowest-correlation sample ---\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` --- Diagnostic for lowest-correlation sample --- ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} print(qc_diagnostics[FileName == outlier_id]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` FileName Protein_Count NA_Percent Avg_Correlation 1: GA_9 8730 2.16295 0.9878816 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} p_diag <- ggplot(qc_diagnostics, aes(x = Protein_Count, y = Avg_Correlation)) + geom_point(aes(color = NA_Percent), size = 4) + geom_vline(xintercept = mad_cutoff, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = FileName), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) + scale_color_viridis_c(name = "% Missing") + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Signal vs. Correlation Diagnostic", subtitle = "Red line = 3-MAD cutoff", x = "Total Protein IDs", y = "Average Pearson Correlation (r)") print(p_diag) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## Contaminant check: top-10 protein signal contribution High top-10% (>50%) suggests a very low-complexity sample or dominant contaminant proteins (e.g. keratins, actin). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} calc_top_n_pct <- function(column, n = 10) { sorted_vals <- sort(column, decreasing = TRUE, na.last = TRUE) (sum(sorted_vals[1:n], na.rm = TRUE) / sum(column, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100 } top10_metrics <- apply(quant_matrix, 2, calc_top_n_pct, n = 10) qc_data[, Top10_Percent := top10_metrics[FileName]] qc_data[, Total_Intensity := colSums(quant_matrix, na.rm = TRUE)[FileName]] contaminant_suspects <- qc_data[Top10_Percent > 50, .(FileName, Condition, Protein_Count, Top10_Percent)] cat("Samples where top 10 proteins account for >50% of signal:\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Samples where top 10 proteins account for >50% of signal: ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} print(contaminant_suspects) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Key: Empty data.table (0 rows and 4 cols): FileName,Condition,Protein_Count,Top10_Percent ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} p_top10 <- ggplot(qc_data, aes(x = Protein_Count, y = Top10_Percent)) + geom_point(aes(color = Condition, size = Total_Intensity), alpha = 0.7) + geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + ggrepel::geom_text_repel( aes(label = ifelse(Top10_Percent > 50, FileName, "")), size = 3, max.overlaps = 10 ) + scale_color_manual(values = condition_colours) + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Top-10 Protein Signal Contribution", subtitle = ">50% suggests contamination or extremely low sample complexity", x = "Total Proteins Identified", y = "% Total Intensity from Top 10 Proteins", size = "Total Signal (raw)") print(p_top10) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](01a_initial_qc_spectronaut_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## Identify top contributing proteins in suspect samples ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} if (nrow(contaminant_suspects) > 0) { suspect_cols <- contaminant_suspects$FileName top_ids <- apply(quant_matrix[, suspect_cols, drop = FALSE], 2, function(x) { quant_wide$PG.ProteinGroups[which.max(x)] }) cat("Top protein per suspect sample:\n") print(top_ids) } else { cat("No suspect samples to investigate.\n") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` No suspect samples to investigate. ``` ::: ::: ## Filter out failed samples ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} valid_runs <- meta$FileName final_runs <- setdiff(valid_runs, failed_runs) cat("Original samples: ", length(valid_runs), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Original samples: 16 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Samples dropped (3-MAD): ", length(failed_runs), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Samples dropped (3-MAD): 0 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} cat("Clean samples remaining: ", length(final_runs), "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Clean samples remaining: 16 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} if (length(failed_runs) > 0) print(failed_runs) ``` ::: ## Save objects ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Clean wide protein matrix (proteins × passing samples) clean_quant_wide <- cbind( quant_wide[, .(PG.ProteinGroups)], quant_wide[, ..final_runs] ) # Clean metadata clean_meta <- qc_data[FileName %in% final_runs] # Dropped sample log dropped_log <- qc_data[FileName %in% failed_runs, .(FileName, Condition, Has_Microglia, Protein_Count)] dropped_log[, Reason := "Sub 3-MAD Protein Count"] fwrite(spec_raw, file.path(objects_dir, "clean_spec_raw.csv")) fwrite(clean_meta, file.path(objects_dir, "clean_metadata.csv")) fwrite(clean_quant_wide, file.path(objects_dir, "clean_quant_wide.csv")) fwrite(dropped_log, file.path(objects_dir, "dropped_samples_log.csv")) cat("Objects saved to:", objects_dir, "\n") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Objects saved to: /nemo/lab/destrooperb/home/shared/zanettc/giulia_proteomics/data/processed/run1 ``` ::: :::
